name: Dad's Key Lime Pie
method: If making your own crust you can use either graham crackers pulverized into crumbs, or prepackaged crumbs. I use organic graham crackers and pulverize them for a fresh taste. You can also buy a premade graham cracker crust. In a large bowl, mix graham cracker crumbs with sugars. Pour melted butter into crumb mixture and mix well, it will gather up pretty easily. The butter acts as glue for your crust. Using your fingers press graham cracker crust into an 8-inch pie plate evenly covering the sides and bottom. Go over it again with your fingers pressing firmly so that the crust stays put. You can store the crust in the refrigerator while you mix your filling.

Preheat oven to 350° F. If making a meringue topping, separate egg yolks and whites and set whites aside for now. In a medium-sized bowl, mix beaten egg yolks, condensed milk, and key lime juice. Pour into unbaked pie crust.

(optional) meringue topping: beat egg whites, add cream of tartar, and continue beating. Add sugar, 1 T at a time. Keep beating until meringue becomes glossy and stiff peaks form. Spoon onto key lime filling and seal edges so that meringue doesn't shrink. Bake on middle oven rack until filling is set, about 15-20 minutes and the top is golden. Remove and let cool, then refrigerate for a couple of hours. Garnish with key lime slices or you can skip the meringue step and bake key lime pie at 350° for 15-20 minutes, or until top sets. Let cool completely then whip 1 cup of heavy cream for the top as I did in the picture, and garnish with lime slices. Serves 4-6. Olé
My dad moved to Key West, Florida in the mid-80s, and he loved key lime pie. I only made it for him a couple of times, but it was his favorite! I make this pie in honor of my dad!
